title: Investigation Master File
description: Canonical data schema for an investigation. Every claim must trace to graded evidence; every entity must have a role; every missing record must be a tracked gap. Agents should maintain the master file as JSON conforming to this schema (e.g. master-file.json in the investigation workspace) and render markdown tables from it, not the other way around.

central_question.description: The single falsifiable question the investigation must answer.

scope_exclusions.description: What this investigation explicitly does NOT cover — prevents scope creep.

legal_hold.description: When true (the default), NOTHING in the investigation workspace may be deleted, overwritten, or redacted in place — evidence, notes, drafts, dead-end leads included. Anticipated litigation makes spoliation the single largest avoidable legal exposure.

evidence_dir.description: Write-once directory for preserved originals, e.g. evidence/. Files are added, never modified or removed.

aliases.description: AKAs, maiden names, DBA names, prior corporate names, common misspellings.

jurisdictions.description: States/countries of registration, residence, or operation — drives which registries to sweep.

identifiers.description: Registry keys for deduplication and record sweeps.

relationships.description: First-class edges between entities. A claim about a connection is only as strong as the documented relationship behind it.

value.description: Monetary amount, ownership %, or other quantification if known.

evidence_ids.description: Empty array = inferred relationship; must be flagged, never published.

reliability.description: Admiralty source-reliability grade. A=completely reliable (certified official record), B=usually reliable, C=fairly reliable, D=not usually reliable, E=unreliable, F=cannot be judged.

credibility.description: Admiralty information-credibility grade. 1=confirmed by independent sources, 2=probably true, 3=possibly true, 4=doubtful, 5=improbable, 6=cannot be judged.

custody.description: Chain of custody — required for libel defense and source protection.

obtained_from.description: Registry URL, agency, or source codename. NEVER a confidential source's real identity — keep that off-file.

hash.description: SHA-256 of the file as obtained, to prove non-alteration.

local_path.description: Path under evidence_dir to the preserved original. Required once retrieved — a citation to a live URL alone is NOT preservation.

archived_url.description: Independent third-party capture (Wayback Machine / archive.today) for any web-sourced item, taken at collection time. Web pages change and disappear; archive immediately, not at publication.

preservation_status.description: An evidence item may not support a publishable claim until 'preserved'. 'unpreservable' (e.g. field observation) requires a contemporaneous dated memo filed as its own DOC- item.

contradicts.description: IDs of evidence or claims this document conflicts with.

text.description: Stated in zero-error procedural form: who, what, when, per which document.

publishable.description: True only if status is 'corroborated' (two independent sources) OR single-source where the source is an A1/A2-graded official record.

defamation_risk.description: High = imputes crime, fraud, or professional incompetence to an identifiable living person/active company. Requires legal review and an on-record comment request before publication.

comment_requested.description: Whether the subject was offered right of reply.

date.description: ISO date or date range; precision flag in date_precision.

strategy.description: FOIA target, registry, alternative route (adversary litigation, state-level parallel request, archive).

collection_log.description: APPEND-ONLY audit trail of every evidentiary action. Entries are never edited or deleted — corrections are logged as new entries referencing the old one. This log is the outlet's proof of good-faith process in any libel or discovery proceeding.

seq.description: Monotonic sequence number; gaps indicate tampering.

actor.description: Agent or human who performed the action.

refs.description: DOC-/CLM-/ENT-/LEAD- ids touched by this action.

notes.description: What was done and why — including searches that returned nothing (negative results are evidence of diligence).

leads.description: Hunches and tips. Leads are NOT claims — they carry no evidentiary weight and must never appear in a draft. Dead-end leads are marked, never deleted.
